pack_status: DEMO KNOWLEDGE PACK — summaries with source metadata for demonstration; every item requires HR/legal verification against the official source before production use

items.title: Normal working hours (private sector)
items.summary: Normal working time for private-sector employees is up to 8 hours per day or 48 hours per week, with reductions applied during Ramadan. Overtime rules and caps apply beyond normal hours.
items.source: Federal Decree-Law No. 33 of 2021 on the Regulation of Labour Relations (as amended) and its implementing resolutions
items.scope: Mainland private-sector employment; free zones may have their own regulations

items.title: Annual leave entitlement
items.summary: Employees are entitled to 30 calendar days of annual leave per year after completing one year of service, and 2 days per month for service between 6 months and 1 year. Employer approval and scheduling rules apply.

items.title: Sick leave entitlement
items.summary: After the probation period, employees may take up to 90 days of sick leave per year, structured in tiers: first 15 days at full pay, next 30 days at half pay, remaining 45 days unpaid. Medical certification requirements apply.

items.title: Maternity, parental, and other statutory leaves
items.summary: Statutory leaves include maternity leave (60 days: 45 at full pay, 15 at half pay), 5 working days of parental leave within 6 months of birth, and bereavement and study leaves in defined cases. Conditions and documentation requirements apply.

items.title: Probation period rules
items.summary: Probation may not exceed 6 months. During probation, termination by the employer requires 14 days written notice; employees leaving to join another UAE employer generally owe 1 month notice. An employee may not be placed on probation more than once with the same employer.

items.title: Termination and notice periods
items.summary: Employment may be terminated for legitimate reasons with written notice between 30 and 90 days as agreed in the contract. End-of-service gratuity, final settlement, and cancellation procedures apply. Unlawful termination can create compensation liability. ALL TERMINATION DECISIONS REQUIRE LEGAL / HR REVIEW.

items.title: Employee records and files
items.summary: Employers must maintain employee files and records (contracts, wage records, leave records) and retain them for the period required by regulation after the end of service. Wage payments run through the approved wage protection channels.
items.source: Federal Decree-Law No. 33 of 2021 (as amended) and implementing resolutions

items.title: ALSAKKAF Systems manager escalation rule
items.summary: Managers and AI Partners must escalate to the Founder with a LEGAL / HR REVIEW REQUIRED flag: termination, discipline, salary decisions, legal interpretation, disputes, medical or personal-sensitive matters, and anything where policy and law appear to conflict. Approved company policy is applied first; where it is silent, the current official legal source is referenced — and a human decides.
items.source: ALSAKKAF HOLDING GROUP internal policy (this pack)
items.scope: All ALSAKKAF Systems managers and AI Partners
